Table of ContentsThis document covers the full product specification for Pulse 360, including platform architecture, user roles, product workflows, cross-product data flows, governance, and the reporting layer.1. PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 3
1. Executive SummaryPulse 360 is an enterprise-grade Marketing Operations Platform that connects every stage of the marketing lifecycle — from strategic brand profiling through to campaign execution and impact measurement — within a single, unified SaaS environment.The platform is designed for marketing agencies that manage multiple clients and brands. Each client can access a tailored set of products, with role-based permissions controlling what each user can see and do. All five product modules share a common data layer, ensuring that brand intelligence, planning data, execution metrics, and measurement insights are always connected.Core Value PropositionPulse 360 eliminates the fragmentation typical in enterprise marketing stacks by providing a single platform where strategy, intelligence, execution, and measurement operate as one connected system — not five disconnected tools.Product Suite at a GlanceModulePrimary FunctionPrimary UsersPulse PlanMedia planning, budget allocation, approval workflowsMedia Planners, ManagersPulse ScoutMarket & competitor intelligence, keyword monitoringIntelligence AnalystsPulse EngageCampaign execution, audience targeting, performance trackingCampaign ManagersPulse ShiftSurvey creation, audience research, impact measurementResearch AnalystsBrand BookBrand profiling, positioning, audience & messaging dataBrand Strategists

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 4
2. Platform Purpose & VisionMarketing operations in enterprise environments are complex. Agencies coordinate across multiple clients and brands simultaneously, managing high-volume campaigns that span digital, broadcast, and out-of-home channels. Research and measurement are often siloed from planning, and campaign intelligence rarely feeds back into strategy in real time.Pulse 360 was created to solve this systemic fragmentation. The platform provides:•A continuous marketing loop from brand knowledge to strategy to execution to insights.•A single source of truth for brand data, shared across all planning, execution, and measurement activities.•Multi-tenancy support so agencies can manage hundreds of clients and brands on one platform.•Role-based access control ensuring every user sees exactly the information and tools relevant to their function.•A credit-based consumption model for survey and campaign execution that gives clients predictable, measurable usage.Marketing LifecycleBrand Knowledge  →  Strategy  →  Market Intelligence  →  Campaign Execution  →  Impact Measurement  →  Insights  →  Strategy Optimisation  →  (repeat)

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 5
3. Platform Architecture & HierarchyEvery action in Pulse 360 is governed by a strict organisational hierarchy. This hierarchy determines what a user can see, which products they can access, and what actions they can perform.3.1 Hierarchy LevelsLevelDescriptionKey AttributesPlatformThe top-level Pulse 360 environmentManaged by Super Admin; controls all global settingsAgencyA marketing agency using the platformCan manage multiple clients; controls product accessClientA brand owner or advertiser managed by the agencyCan have multiple brands; assigned specific productsBrandA distinct brand identity within a clientHas its own Brand Book, plans, campaigns, and surveysProduct AccessWhich modules are enabled for a client/brandConfigurable per client; can be enabled/disabled at any timeProduct RoleA user's role within a specific productDetermines read/write/approve permissionsFeature PermissionsGranular controls within each productControls visibility of specific features and data3.2 Hierarchy FlowAccess CascadePulse 360 Platform  →  Agency  →  Client  →  Brand  →  Product Access  →  Product Role  →  Feature Permissions  Each level inherits constraints from the level above. An Agency Admin cannot grant permissions that exceed their own. A Client Admin cannot enable products that the Agency has not assigned.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 6
4. User Roles & Permissions4.1 Platform-Level RolesRoleResponsibilitiesSuper AdminPlatform owner. Manages agencies, product settings, feature flags, global reporting, and billing. Has unrestricted access to all modules.Agency AdminManages all clients and users within their agency. Controls product access, credit allocation, and governance configuration. Cannot access other agencies' data.Client AdminRepresents a client organisation. Manages client users, brand settings, and product roles. Cannot access agency-level settings.4.2 Product-Level RolesEach product module has its own set of roles. A user may hold different roles in different products.Pulse PlanRoleCapabilitiesMedia PlannerCreates and edits media plans; selects channels and allocates budget; uploads rate cards; submits plans for approval.Manager / CSTReviews submitted plans; approves or rejects with comments; locks budgets; oversees overall planning activity.Client (Viewer)Views approved plans and exports reports. Cannot edit or create plans.Pulse ScoutRoleCapabilitiesScout AdminConfigures monitoring campaigns; sets keywords, target websites, and frequency; has full dashboard access.EditorCan edit monitoring configurations and view dashboards.ViewerRead-only access to dashboards and trend reports.Pulse EngageRoleCapabilitiesCampaign AdminFull access to create, launch, pause, and archive campaigns. Manages team access within the product.Campaign CreatorCreates and edits campaigns; cannot launch without approval; can

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 7
view performance dashboards.Pulse ShiftRoleCapabilitiesSurvey AdminManages survey credits; creates, publishes, and archives surveys; views all response data and analytics.Survey CreatorCreates and edits surveys; launches surveys within credit limits; views own survey results.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 8
5. Login & Access FlowThe login and session management system ensures every user lands in the correct context immediately upon authentication.5.1 Authentication Steps1.User navigates to the Pulse 360 login page or SSO endpoint.2.System validates credentials against the user directory (email, password, or SSO token).3.On successful validation, the system checks: user status (active/inactive), organisation assignment, MFA if enabled.4.Session is created and the system loads the user's context: agency, client, brand access, product access, and role per product.5.User is routed to their default landing experience based on their highest-level role.5.2 Landing Experience by RoleRoleDefault LandingSuper AdminPlatform-level dashboard showing global usage, active agencies, revenue metrics, and system health.Agency AdminAgency dashboard showing client overview, active campaigns, credit usage, and team activity.Client AdminClient dashboard showing brand-level metrics, active plans, and product usage summary.Product UsersProduct launcher screen showing cards for all assigned modules.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 9
6. Product LauncherThe Product Launcher is the central hub that users see after login if they do not have a higher-level admin role. It provides access to all assigned modules from a single, consistent interface.6.1 Launcher InterfaceThe launcher displays a card for each product the user has been assigned. Cards that are not assigned are hidden — they do not appear as locked or greyed out. Each visible card includes:•Product name and icon.•A short description of the product's purpose.•Current access status (e.g. Active, Trial, Suspended).•A prominent Launch button to open the product.6.2 Products Available in LauncherProductDescriptionTypical UsersPulse PlanCreate, manage, and approve media plans across all channels.Media Planners, ManagersPulse ScoutMonitor competitor activity, keyword trends, and market signals.Intelligence AnalystsPulse EngageBuild and launch campaigns, track performance in real time.Campaign ManagersPulse ShiftDesign surveys, collect responses, and measure campaign impact.Research AnalystsBrand BookAccess and update the brand's strategic knowledge base.Brand Strategists, All RolesContext PersistenceWhen a user switches from the launcher into any product, their current brand context is carried forward. They do not need to re-select their brand on each product entry.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 10
7. Client OnboardingClient onboarding is the process by which an agency sets up a new client on the platform and configures their access to the Pulse 360 suite.7.1 Onboarding StepsStep 1 — Create Client RecordThe Agency Admin creates the client record with the following information:•Client name and industry vertical.•Primary website URL.•Key contact person (name, email, phone).•Contract start and end dates.•Billing details (if applicable).Step 2 — Create BrandsWithin the client record, the admin creates one or more brand profiles. Each brand requires:•Brand name and category.•Target market or geography.•Brand owner (internal contact).•Initial Brand Book population (can be completed later by brand strategist).Step 3 — Enable ProductsThe Agency Admin selects which Pulse 360 modules the client is licensed to use. Products not enabled remain invisible to the client's users. Each product can be enabled or disabled at any time.Step 4 — Create Users & Assign RolesFor each user to be added, the admin specifies:•Full name and email address.•Role at the client level (Client Admin or Product User).•Product access and product-level role for each enabled module.•Brand access (which brands the user can see within the client).Step 5 — Allocate CreditsFor Pulse Shift and Pulse Engage, the admin assigns a credit budget to the client. Credits can be further sub-allocated to individual users or teams.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 11
8. Brand Book — Brand Knowledge LayerBrand Book is the central brand intelligence layer of the Pulse 360 suite. It acts as the single source of truth for brand data, and all other products draw from it when they need brand context.8.1 Brand Book SectionsSectionContentBrand OverviewHigh-level description of the brand — its category, heritage, market position, and key facts.Mission & VisionThe brand's purpose statement and long-term vision for its market.Brand PillarsThe core values and principles that define how the brand behaves and communicates.CompetitorsA structured list of direct and indirect competitors, with notes on positioning differences.Tone & MessagingApproved language, tone guidelines, key messages, and phrases to use and avoid.Audience SegmentsDefined audience personas including demographics, psychographics, and channel preferences.KeywordsPrimary and secondary search keywords associated with the brand, used by Pulse Scout and Pulse Plan.8.2 How Other Products Use Brand Book•Pulse Plan pulls brand keywords and audience segments to inform channel selection and targeting.•Pulse Scout uses brand keywords and competitor data to configure monitoring campaigns automatically.•Pulse Engage uses audience segment data to pre-populate campaign targeting parameters.•Pulse Shift uses brand pillars and audience segments to suggest relevant survey questions.•The Reporting Engine uses brand metadata for filtering and benchmarking reports.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 12
9. Pulse Plan — Media PlanningPulse Plan is the media planning module of the Pulse 360 suite. It enables media planners to build comprehensive, multi-channel media plans and route them through a structured approval process.9.1 Core WorkflowStep 1 — Select Brand ContextThe planner selects the brand they are creating the plan for. Brand Book data (keywords, audience segments) is automatically loaded into the planning context.Step 2 — Select ChannelsThe planner chooses from available media channels:•Digital: Google Search, Google Display, Meta (Facebook/Instagram), Programmatic.•Broadcast: TV (national, regional), Radio.•Out of Home (OOH): Billboards, Transit, Digital OOH.Step 3 — Budget AllocationThe planner distributes the total campaign budget across selected channels. The system provides allocation guidance based on historical performance benchmarks.Step 4 — Upload Rate CardsMedia cost rate cards are uploaded per channel. The system uses these to calculate estimated reach, impressions, and cost-per-metric for each channel.Step 5 — Forecast ResultsBased on the budget and rate cards, the system generates:•Estimated reach by channel.•Total impressions.•Estimated ROI benchmarks.•GRP (Gross Rating Points) for TV and Radio.Step 6 — Submit for ApprovalThe completed plan is submitted to the assigned manager for review. The plan enters a pending approval state.9.2 Approval WorkflowActionDescriptionManager: ReviewReviews the submitted plan in full; can add comments at line-item level.Manager: ApproveApproves the plan, making it visible to the client stakeholder.Manager: RejectRejects with mandatory comments explaining required changes. Plan

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 13
is returned to planner for revision.Manager: Lock BudgetLocks the plan budget to prevent further changes after approval.Client: ViewClient stakeholder can view the approved plan and add comments.Client: ExportClient can export the plan as PDF or Excel for internal distribution.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 14
10. Pulse Scout — Market IntelligencePulse Scout is the intelligence and governance module. It provides continuous monitoring of competitor activity, keyword trends, and broader market signals, giving brands a real-time view of their competitive landscape.10.1 Core WorkflowStep 1 — Configure Monitoring CampaignThe analyst creates a monitoring campaign by defining:•Campaign name and brand context.•Target keywords (drawn from Brand Book or entered manually).•Target websites and domains to monitor (competitor sites, news portals, industry publications).•Geographic scope of monitoring.•Monitoring frequency (real-time, daily, weekly).•Campaign duration (start and end dates).Step 2 — Governance ValidationBefore the monitoring campaign is activated, the system validates it against the governance rules configured by the Agency Admin:•Keyword count must not exceed the configured limit.•Campaign duration must fall within permitted ranges.•Targeting scope must not exceed the approved geography.•Campaign cost must be within the budget allocated.If any rule is violated, the campaign is blocked and the user is shown a specific governance violation message.Step 3 — Data CollectionOnce active, Scout continuously collects:•Competitor ad campaigns (creative, copy, placements, platforms).•Keyword search volume trends.•Ad placement intelligence (where competitors are appearing).•Market signals from monitored domains.Step 4 — Intelligence DashboardCollected data is surfaced in an interactive dashboard showing:•Keyword trend charts (volume over time).•Competitor activity feed (new campaigns detected).•Share-of-voice comparison.•Top performing ad creatives from competitors.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 15
•Geographic distribution of competitor activity.10.2 Governance RulesAgency Admins configure governance rules at the agency or client level. Rules include:Rule TypeDescriptionKeyword LimitMaximum number of keywords permitted per monitoring campaign.Budget CapMaximum spend per campaign period; system blocks campaigns that exceed this.Duration LimitMaximum and minimum allowable campaign durations.Targeting RestrictionsGeographic regions or content categories that are restricted from monitoring.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 16
11. Pulse Engage — Campaign ExecutionPulse Engage is the campaign execution module. It enables campaign managers to build, launch, and monitor marketing campaigns directly within the Pulse 360 platform, using a credit-based consumption model.11.1 Core WorkflowStep 1 — Create CampaignThe campaign manager creates a new campaign by specifying:•Campaign name and brand context.•Objective (awareness, consideration, conversion, retention).•Channel (digital channels connected via the platform's integrations).•Creative assets (uploaded or selected from the asset library).•Audience targeting (drawn from Brand Book audience segments or manually defined).•Campaign budget and flight dates.Step 2 — Credit ValidationBefore the campaign can be submitted for launch, the system checks:•The campaign's credit cost (calculated from audience size, duration, and channel).•Available credits in the client's account.•If sufficient credits are available, the campaign moves to the launch stage. If not, the manager is prompted to request a credit top-up.Step 3 — Launch CampaignOn launch, the campaign is activated and credits are deducted from the client's balance. The system records the activation timestamp and begins collecting performance data.Step 4 — Performance MonitoringThe live performance dashboard displays:•Real-time impressions and reach.•Engagement rate (clicks, interactions).•Conversion events.•Cost per result (CPM, CPC, CPA as applicable).•Pacing against budget (daily spend vs. plan).11.2 Campaign StatesStateDescriptionDraftCampaign is being built; not yet submitted for review.Pending ApprovalCampaign submitted; awaiting Campaign Admin approval.ApprovedCampaign approved and scheduled for launch.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 17
ActiveCampaign is live; performance data is being collected.PausedCampaign temporarily suspended; credits are not being consumed.CompletedCampaign has reached its end date; final report generated.ArchivedCampaign closed and stored for historical reference.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 18
12. Pulse Shift — Surveys & ResearchPulse Shift is the measurement and research module. It enables research analysts to design surveys, deploy them to defined audiences, collect responses, and generate insights that measure brand health and campaign effectiveness.12.1 Core WorkflowStep 1 — Create SurveyThe analyst creates a survey by defining:•Survey name and brand context.•Survey type (brand health tracker, campaign impact study, audience insight, custom).•Question set (multiple choice, rating scales, open text, semantic differential).•Target audience criteria (pulled from Brand Book audience segments or defined manually).•Desired sample size.Step 2 — Credit ValidationThe system calculates the credit cost based on sample size and audience complexity. The analyst is shown the estimated cost before launch. If credits are sufficient, the survey moves to the ready state.Step 3 — Launch SurveyOn launch, the survey is distributed to the target audience panel. Credits are deducted based on responses collected (not upfront).Step 4 — Response CollectionThe live response dashboard shows:•Total responses collected vs. target sample.•Completion rate.•Response breakdown by audience segment.•Real-time charting of key question results.Step 5 — Analysis & ReportingOnce the target sample is reached (or the survey is manually closed), the system generates:•Brand recall score.•Net Sentiment Score.•Campaign impact rating (for post-campaign surveys).•Audience insight report by segment.•Exportable survey report (PDF, Excel).

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 19
13. Cross-Product Data FlowOne of the key differentiators of Pulse 360 is that all five products share a connected data layer. Data produced in one product is available — where appropriate — to all other products and to the reporting engine.Data Flow DirectionBrand Book  →  Pulse Plan  →  Pulse Scout  →  Pulse Engage  →  Pulse Shift  →  Reporting Engine  Data flows both downstream (from strategy to execution) and upstream (from execution results back to Brand Book and strategy).13.1 Data Exchange MapFromToData TransferredBrand BookPulse PlanKeywords, audience segments, competitor list, channel preferencesBrand BookPulse ScoutBrand keywords, competitor domains, monitoring targetsBrand BookPulse EngageAudience segment definitions, targeting parametersBrand BookPulse ShiftAudience personas, brand pillars for survey designPulse PlanPulse EngageApproved budget by channel, flight dates, campaign objectivesPulse PlanReporting EnginePlanned budget, forecasted reach, approval timestampsPulse ScoutPulse PlanCompetitor activity insights to inform planning decisionsPulse ScoutReporting EngineMarket signal data, keyword trend indices, competitor metricsPulse EngagePulse ShiftExposed audience data for post-campaign survey targetingPulse EngageReporting EngineActual spend, impressions, engagement, conversion dataPulse ShiftBrand BookUpdated audience insights, brand recall scores, sentiment dataPulse ShiftReporting EngineSurvey results, brand health scores, campaign impact ratings

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 20
14. Reporting LayerThe Reporting Engine aggregates data from all five product modules into a unified intelligence layer. Reports are available at platform, agency, client, and brand levels.14.1 Reporting LevelsLevelMetrics AvailablePlatform Reporting (Super Admin)Global product adoption, total revenue, system-wide usage statistics, agency-level benchmarks.Agency Reporting (Agency Admin)Client performance summary, total budget under management, campaign volume, credit consumption across clients.Client Reporting (Client Admin)Brand-level metrics, plans created, active campaigns, survey completions, budget vs. actuals.Brand Reporting (All Roles)Campaign performance, keyword trends, brand health scores, competitor share-of-voice.14.2 Unified Dashboard MetricsThe unified reporting dashboard includes the following standard metric groups:•Planning: Plans created, budget planned, approval cycle time.•Intelligence: Keyword volume trends, competitor campaign count, market signal index.•Execution: Active campaigns, impressions delivered, engagement rate, conversion rate, cost per result.•Research: Surveys launched, responses collected, brand recall score, net sentiment score.•Platform: Credit consumption, product adoption rate, user activity.14.3 Filtering & SegmentationAll reports can be filtered by:•Agency•Client•Brand•Product module•Date range•Campaign or plan

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 21
15. Credit & Governance System15.1 Credit ModelPulse Shift and Pulse Engage operate on a credit-based consumption model. Credits are purchased by the agency and allocated to clients. Clients can further sub-allocate credits to individual users or teams.ProductCredit Consumption ModelPulse Engage CreditsConsumed per campaign activation. Cost calculated by audience size × channel × duration.Pulse Shift CreditsConsumed per survey response collected. Cost varies by audience panel complexity.Credits are deducted automatically when a campaign launches or a survey response is collected. If a client runs out of credits, active campaigns are paused and new launches are blocked until credits are replenished.15.2 Governance ControlsGovernance rules protect the platform from misuse and ensure compliance with client-defined constraints. Rules are configured by the Agency Admin and applied at the campaign or survey level.Governance RuleEffectBudget CapMaximum allowable campaign spend in a given period.Keyword LimitMaximum number of keywords per Scout monitoring campaign.Targeting RestrictionsBlocked geographies, audience categories, or content classifications.Duration LimitsMinimum and maximum allowed campaign or survey durations.Approval ThresholdsCampaigns or plans above a certain budget threshold require additional approval steps.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 22
16. Audit & LoggingEvery significant action in the Pulse 360 platform is recorded in an immutable audit log. Logs are searchable, filterable, and exportable by authorised users.16.1 Logged Events•Authentication: Login, logout, failed login attempts, SSO events.•User Management: User creation, role changes, deactivation, password resets.•Access Changes: Product access granted or revoked; brand access changes.•Planning Events: Plan created, edited, submitted, approved, rejected, locked.•Campaign Events: Campaign created, launched, paused, resumed, completed.•Survey Events: Survey created, launched, closed, results published.•Credit Events: Credit allocation, deduction, top-up requests.•Governance Events: Governance rule violations, blocked campaigns, rule changes.16.2 Log AccessRoleLog Access ScopeSuper AdminAccess to all logs across all agencies and clients.Agency AdminAccess to logs for their agency and all clients within it.Client AdminAccess to logs for their client only.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 23
17. Product Navigation & SwitchingPulse 360 provides seamless navigation between all product modules without requiring re-authentication. The top navigation bar is present across all products.17.1 Top Navigation BarThe top navigation bar contains:•Pulse 360 logo / home link (returns to Product Launcher).•Product Switcher: dropdown or tab bar for Pulse Plan, Pulse Scout, Pulse Engage, Pulse Shift, Brand Book, and Reports.•Brand context indicator: shows the currently active brand; clicking it opens the brand selector.•Notifications icon: shows alerts for pending approvals, governance violations, and credit warnings.•User avatar / menu: access to profile settings, help, and logout.17.2 Context PersistenceWhen a user switches between products, the platform preserves:•The currently selected agency and client.•The currently active brand.•The user's authentication session.This eliminates repetitive context selection and reduces friction in cross-product workflows.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 24
18. Core System ServicesAll five product modules are built on a shared set of platform services. These services ensure consistency, security, and scalability across the entire suite.ServiceFunctionAuthentication ServiceHandles login, SSO, session management, and MFA. Integrates with enterprise identity providers (SAML, OAuth 2.0).User DirectoryCentral store of all users, roles, and permissions. Single source of truth for access control.Client & Brand ServiceManages the agency → client → brand hierarchy and all associated metadata.Permission EngineEvaluates access requests in real time against role and product configurations. Enforces hierarchy rules.Reporting EngineAggregates data from all product modules. Generates cross-product dashboards, standard reports, and exports.Credit ServiceTracks credit balances, allocations, deductions, and top-up requests across all clients.Governance ServiceStores and evaluates governance rules. Blocks non-compliant campaigns and logs violations.Audit Log ServiceRecords all platform events with immutable timestamps, actor identity, and change details.Notification ServiceDelivers in-platform alerts, email notifications, and webhook events for integrations.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 25
19. Real-World User JourneyThe following end-to-end example illustrates how a full marketing campaign lifecycle plays out across all five products on the Pulse 360 platform.StepActorAction & Outcome1. Client SetupAgency AdminCreates a new client record for 'Acme Consumer Goods'. Adds two brands: AcmeFresh and AcmePro. Enables all five modules. Allocates 500 Engage credits and 2,000 Shift credits.2. Brand ProfilingBrand StrategistOpens Brand Book for AcmeFresh. Enters brand mission, positioning pillars, audience segments (Health-Conscious 25-40, Budget Families), competitor list, and primary keywords. Saves to the platform.3. Media PlanningMedia PlannerOpens Pulse Plan. Selects AcmeFresh. Uses Brand Book audience data to select Google, Meta, and OOH channels. Allocates $200K budget. Uploads rate cards. Reviews forecasted 4.2M reach. Submits plan.4. Plan ApprovalManagerReviews plan in Pulse Plan. Approves with a comment to increase OOH allocation by 10%. Budget is locked. Client stakeholder is notified.5. Market IntelligenceIntelligence AnalystOpens Pulse Scout. Creates a monitoring campaign using AcmeFresh keywords from Brand Book. Sets competitor domains. Launches monitoring. Within 48 hours, detects that primary competitor has increased spend on Google by 40%.6. Campaign LaunchCampaign ManagerOpens Pulse Engage. Creates a campaign aligned to the approved plan. Audience targeting auto-populated from Brand Book. Deducts 120 credits on launch. Campaign goes live across Google and Meta.7. Impact MeasurementResearch AnalystOpens Pulse Shift. Creates a post-campaign brand lift survey targeting AcmeFresh audience segments. Sets 500-response sample. Launches survey. 500 responses collected over 3 days. Brand recall improved 8 points.8. ReportingClient AdminOpens the Reports module. Views the unified client dashboard showing: $200K planned vs. $194K actual spend, 4.1M impressions delivered, 3.2% engagement rate, 8-point brand recall lift. Exports full report as PDF.

PULSE 360  |  Product Specification    CONFIDENTIAL
Pulse 360 — Marketing Operations PlatformPage 26
20. GlossaryTermDefinitionAgencyA marketing services company that uses Pulse 360 to manage campaigns on behalf of its clients.BrandA distinct brand identity within a client organisation, with its own Brand Book, plans, and campaigns.Brand BookThe centralised brand knowledge module that stores all strategic brand data for use across the suite.CreditA unit of consumption used by Pulse Engage and Pulse Shift to meter campaign and survey activity.GovernancePlatform-enforced rules that limit campaign spend, targeting, duration, and other parameters.Media PlanA structured document in Pulse Plan outlining channel selection, budget allocation, and reach forecasts.Monitoring CampaignA Scout configuration that continuously collects market and competitor intelligence.Product LauncherThe central dashboard that users see after login, showing all their assigned product modules.ROIReturn on Investment — the estimated or actual return from a media investment.SSOSingle Sign-On — allows users to authenticate once and access all Pulse 360 products without re-logging in.Super AdminThe platform owner role with unrestricted access to all agencies, clients, and system settings.SurveyA research instrument in Pulse Shift used to collect audience feedback and measure brand or campaign performance.— END OF DOCUMENT —Pulse 360 Product Specification  |  Version 1.0  |  Confidential
